Lights flickering in the old tower, steady like a heartbeat
A sound from the ruins that carried for miles
Every animal in the area went silent at the same moment
The stream ran warm for half a day, then went back to normal
A patch of ground near the crossroads that won't hold frost
The fire in the common hall turned blue for a few minutes, no one touched it
Shadows moving the wrong direction in the late afternoon
A ring of dead grass appeared overnight in the clearing, perfectly round
The bells in the old shrine started ringing on their own
The wind changed direction four times in an hour, then stopped completely
Someone's compass spun in circles near the standing stones and wouldn't settle
The water in the rain barrel was ice cold in the middle of a warm spell
A crack opened in the foundation wall that wasn't there the day before
Birds flew in a tight circle over the village for most of the morning
The stars looked wrong that night, like something shifted overhead
Voices carrying from the hills, but nobody lives up there
Every candle on the street went out at the same time, no wind at all
A deep hum from underground that you could feel in your teeth
Moss growing in patterns on the old wall, almost like writing
The weather turned from clear to hail and back in the span of a meal
A tree at the crossroads split down the middle overnight, no storm
The sundial's shadow fell the wrong way for about an hour
Something left scorch marks on the standing stones, but there was no fire
The old well echoed back a voice that wasn't anyone's
Dust falling from the ceiling beams in the storehouse, rhythmic like footsteps above
A fog that rolled in from the hilltop instead of the valley
Fruit on the market tables rotted in minutes while everyone watched
The metalwork on the gate was warm to the touch at midnight
A flock of crows landed on every rooftop at once, sat for an hour, then left together
The echo in the canyon has been coming back wrong, like it's answering
